summary: Closed, unmerged WIP PR that added many pending C implementations across fighter, stage, item, menu, and audio code. The PR body reported 0 matched and 5 failed targets, but the decomp.dev bot reported 13 new 100% matches and 18 partial improvements, including matches in grcorneria, grhomerun, ftCo_Attack100, ftcolanim, itmewtwoshadowball, ftCrazyHand, lbaudio_ax, and groldpupupu. The slice is useful mainly as a stash of matching tactics and struct/prototype refinements, not as an accepted final review record.

key_files.why: Largest stage change; implemented four near-identical Home Run text-position/update functions and added grHr_804D6AE4.
Added grHomeRun_GroundVars and union member homerun, replacing some generic gv.unk usage with a stage-specific layout.
Implemented three Corneria functions; decomp.dev reported all three as 100% matches.
Added grGb_803E4048 data table and a near-match for grGreatBay_801F660C that updates map vertex positions from JObj transforms.
Implemented grOldPupupu_80210C7C; decomp.dev reported it as a 100% match using s16 field layout and inline HSD_Randi range logic.
Refined static data layout from int to two s16 fields and used a rand_inline helper for a 99.76% improvement.
Implemented ft_800C0098 as a 100% match using pragmas to disable auto inlining and global optimization.
Implemented fn_800DC070; decomp.dev reported a 100% match.
Implemented ftCh_Init_80157170; paired with a type fix in ftCrazyHand attributes and reported as a 100% match.
Changed ftCrazyHandAttributes x24 from s32 to float, supporting the Crazy Hand damage-state implementation.
Implemented ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420 as a partial match involving Kirby hat DObj/MObj material diffuse writes.
Implemented destruction and shield-bounce behavior; destruction was reported as a 100% match.
Changed Mewtwo Shadow Ball item vars so x2C is an HSD_GObj* owner/reference and shifted the float array to x30.
Added several tool item logic functions with repeated collision/absorb state transition patterns, all reported as partial improvements.
Implemented lbAudioAx_80024DC4 and adjusted object allocation access to the new pool struct; function reported as a 100% match.
Expanded lbl_80433710 from HSD_ObjAllocData into lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc with alloc, x2C, and x70 arrays, improving .bss matching.
Added near-match mnName_8023749C with language-dependent name table selection and opt_common_subs disabled.

what_changed: Replaced placeholder comments with C implementations for many functions, especially stage callbacks and item/fighter state handlers.

decomp.dev reported 13 new full matches: grCorneria_801E0F6C, grCorneria_801E2D90, grHomeRun_8021DF50, grHomeRun_8021E0D4, grHomeRun_8021E258, grHomeRun_8021E3DC, grCorneria_801E0D30, fn_800DC070, ft_800C0098, it_2725_Logic101_Destroyed, ftCh_Init_80157170, lbAudioAx_80024DC4, and grOldPupupu_80210C7C.
Added or corrected prototypes in headers such as grGreatBay_801F660C(Ground_GObj*), grHomeRun_8021EC58(u16), it_802EF098(Item_GObj*, s32), and mnName_8023749C(int).
Introduced stage/item/library struct-layout refinements: grHomeRun_GroundVars, lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc, ftCrazyHandAttributes.x24 as float, and itMewtwoShadowball_ItemVars.x2C as HSD_GObj*.
Added data/table declarations such as grGb_803E4048 for Great Bay and mnNameNew_803EE720/mnNameNew_803EE724 externs for name lookup.
Used compiler pragmas around sensitive functions: ft_800C0098 disabled auto_inline and global_optimizer; mnName_8023749C disabled opt_common_subs.
The PR body listed 5 failed pending targets unrelated or not fully solved in the excerpt: grOnett_801E5194, fn_80170110, grPura_80212FC0, IsNameUnique, and pl_80038628.
smart_moves: For ft_800C0098, the implementation deliberately wrapped the function in #pragma auto_inline off and #pragma global_optimizer off, a targeted compiler-control tactic that produced a 100% match according to the bot.
For mnName_8023749C, #pragma opt_common_subs off was used around a small string-table lookup where common-subexpression optimization likely affected codegen.

The lbAudioAx change recognized that lbl_80433710 was not just an allocator but a larger pool/control struct containing an HSD_ObjAllocData at offset 0 plus arrays at x2C and x70; updating allocator calls to lbl_80433710.alloc preserved access to both interpretations.
The Home Run work promoted generic GroundVars_unk fields into grHomeRun_GroundVars with named offsets xC4, xC6, xC8, and xCC, making repeated text-position code easier to type and reason about.
The Mewtwo Shadow Ball item-vars change identified x2C as an HSD_GObj* rather than part of a float array, enabling owner/reference cleanup logic in it_2725_Logic101_Destroyed.
The Old Kongo and Old Pupupu changes narrowed data fields from int to s16 pairs, matching random range behavior and improving codegen.
The Great Bay implementation used a compact s16 pair table to map vertex IDs to JObj IDs, then asserted the JObj and propagated world positions into mpVtxSetPos.
decomp_lessons: Unmerged WIP PRs can still contain high-value matching examples, but their code should be reused only after checking current master and rerunning matching because this PR was closed without merge.

When a global is used both as an allocator and as indexed state, model the enclosing struct and update allocator calls to the nested alloc field instead of forcing the whole global to be HSD_ObjAllocData.
Stage ground-variable unions benefit from stage-specific structs once repeated xC4/xC8/xCC accesses appear; this can improve both readability and matchability.
Small type corrections, such as s32 to float or int to s16, can be decisive for matching motion-state calls and random-range code.
Repeated collision/item response handlers may intentionally duplicate logic rather than factoring helpers if the original code duplicated it; decomp matching should preserve that shape.
For functions sensitive to compiler scheduling or optimization, local pragmas are an acceptable matching tactic when documented by the diff and kept narrowly scoped.
Data tables should be typed to the narrowest observed element width when the generated loads/sign-extension matter, as shown by grGb_803E4048 and the Old Kongo/Pupupu s16 layouts.
Pointer-versus-array corrections in item vars can unlock semantic cleanup functions, especially owner references and callbacks into fighter-special state reset routines.
naming_conventions: Stage-specific globals use short module prefixes such as grHr_ for Home Run, grGb_ for Great Bay, grOk_ for Old Kongo, and grOp_ for Old Pupupu.
New stage-specific ground vars followed the pattern struct grHomeRun_GroundVars and union member homerun.
Unknown or address-derived functions retained project-standard address names such as fn_800DC070, ftCh_Init_80157170, and it_802EF098 when no semantic name was evidenced.
Header prototypes were upgraded from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures once argument and return types were inferred.
Offset-style temporary field names such as xC4, xC6, xC8, xCC, x2C, and x70 were retained where semantics were still weak.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use #pragma push/#pragma pop to localize compiler setting changes around one function.
Disable auto inlining and global optimizer for ft_800C0098 to match original code shape.
Disable common-subexpression optimization for mnName_8023749C to preserve desired lookup/codegen behavior.
Model allocator-containing globals as wrapper structs when later offsets are accessed directly; then call HSD_ObjAlloc/HSD_ObjFree/HSD_ObjAllocInit on the nested alloc member.
Use explicit casts for overlayed data, such as casting gp->gv.unk.xCC to HSD_JObj* and gp->gv.unk.xC8 to HSD_Text* in Home Run functions.
Preserve unusual arithmetic and zero multiplications in Home Run text positioning, e.g. adding 0.0F * (grHr_804D6AE4 * scale), because such expressions can affect generated instruction sequences.
Use loop structures and post-decrement comparisons as seen in grCorneria_801E0F6C to match state timers.
Represent material color writes through raw u32 casts when the original appears to copy packed diffuse color data, as in ftKb_SpecialN_800F1420.
Normalize angles with while loops against 0.0f and M_TAU in it_2725_Logic101_ShieldBounced rather than replacing with higher-level modulo helpers.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
The PR body reported progress as 0 matched and 5 failed: grOnett_801E5194 at 78.1%, fn_80170110 no match, grPura_80212FC0 at 96.4%, IsNameUnique at 97.1%, and pl_80038628 at 55.6%.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 58.65%, +0.06%, +2200 bytes.

The bot listed 13 new 100% matches and 18 improvements in unmatched items, so the automated report contradicts the PR body's simple '0 matched' status and is the stronger evidence for functions in the diff.
Because the PR was closed with merged_at null, none of these changes should be treated as accepted reviewer-endorsed solutions without revalidation.
follow_up_queries: Check whether any functions from PR #2315 were later merged in separate PRs, especially the 13 bot-reported 100% matches.
Search current master for ft_800C0098 and mnName_8023749C to see whether the same pragma tactics survived review.
Compare current lbAudioAx static data layout against lbAudioAx_PoolAlloc from this PR before reusing the struct.
Revalidate grHomeRun_GroundVars field names and types against later Home Run stage work.
Investigate the five PR-body failed targets: grOnett_801E5194, fn_80170110, grPura_80212FC0, IsNameUnique, and pl_80038628.
Look for later review discussion on whether repeated Home Run functions should remain duplicated or be abstracted only after matching.
